agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Richmond Council to explore funding for student scholarships
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will receive a presentation from Richmond Promise, a local organization that helps Richmond students access and complete post-secondary education (including college, trade school, and university). After the presentation, Council will consider directing staff to explore funding options to support scholarships for Richmond youth. This is a proposed step only — no funding has been approved yet.
agenda_briefs.action_required: No action needed from business owners at this time. Watch for the council decision on funding.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
Q.2
Presentation from Richmond Promise: Helping to build a stronger Richmond by deeply supporting Richmond students to access and successfully complete post-secondary education (not only 4 year college)
•  RECEIVE a presentation from Richmond Promise on the organization’s current scope of services, how it supports youth before, during, and after post-secondary education, and the impact it has achieved to date and to highlight the program’s current needs as it continues to serve Richmond students; and DIRECT staff to explore funding to support scholarships for Richmond youth - Vice Mayor Doria Robinson (510-620-6568).

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Richmond Council to oppose Montezuma CO2 pipeline project
agenda_briefs.summary: Richmond City Council is set to vote on a resolution opposing the proposed Montezuma Carbon Sequestration Hub. This project would involve building a large network of CO2 pipelines through Richmond, Contra Costa, and Solano Counties to transport and store carbon waste underground. Council will hear public comments and deliberate on whether to formally oppose the project, citing risks to the health and safety of Richmond residents. A decision has not yet been made.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Attend the council meeting or submit public comment to voice your support or opposition before the vote.
agenda_briefs.industries: Natural Resources, Transportation & Logistics, Construction & Development
agenda_briefs.source_body:
Q.1
Opposition to Proposed CO2 Waste Dumping Projects & CO2 Pipelines in Richmond, Contra Costa, and Solano Counties
RECEIVE a presentation on proposed Montezuma CO2 Hub, a carbon waste dumping project, that would require a massive build out of pipelines through Richmond, risking the health and safety of Richmond residents and ADOPT a resolution that opposes the proposed Montezuma Carbon Sequestration Hub's CO2 pipeline waste dumping projects and CO2 pipelines in Richmond, Contra Costa, and Solano Counties - Councilmember Claudia Jimenez (510-620-6565). This item was continued from the April 28, 2026, and May 5, 2026, meeting. THE PRESENTATION FOR THIS ITEM WAS RECEIVED ON MAY 5, 2026. THIS ITEM IS FOR PUBLIC COMMENT AND COUNCIL DELIBERATION ONLY.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Richmond council to hold public hearing on city vacancies
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider holding a public hearing and receiving a presentation on the status of city vacancies and the city's recruitment and retention efforts in 2025. This is a proposed step — it has not been approved yet. The hearing would let the public and business owners learn about staffing gaps at City Hall and how the city plans to fill them.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Attend the public hearing to learn how city vacancies may affect services your business relies on.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
P.4
Annual Public Hearing on Vacancies
•  HOLD a public hearing and RECEIVE a presentation on the status of vacancies and the City’s recruitment and retention efforts in 2025 – Human Resources (Nickie Mastay/Sharrone Taylor 510-620-6803).

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Richmond council to vote on new Master Fee Schedule
agenda_briefs.summary: Richmond City Council is set to hold a public hearing and introduce a proposed ordinance (a local law) that would establish a new Master Fee Schedule. This schedule sets the fees the city charges for various services, permits, and licences. The proposal is up for a first reading and has not been approved yet. If passed, it could change what you pay for city services.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Attend the public hearing to voice your concerns or support before council votes on the fee changes.

key_dates.label: Richmond Master Fee Schedule public hearing

agenda_briefs.source_body:
P.3
Master Fee Schedule Update
•  HOLD a public hearing; and INTRODUCE an ordinance (first reading) establishing certain fees for the proposed Master Fee Schedule – Finance Department (Emily Combs/Mubeen Qader/Antonio Banuelos 510-620-6741).

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Richmond to vote on special assessments for unpaid fines
agenda_briefs.summary: Richmond Council is set to vote on a proposal that would allow the city to add unpaid administrative citation fees and nuisance abatement costs directly onto property tax bills as a 'special assessment'. This means if a property owner fails to pay fines for things like bylaw violations or cleaning up a nuisance property, the city could recover that money by placing a charge on the property's taxes. A public hearing will be held before the vote. This is not yet approved — it is up for discussion and a decision at the next council meeting.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Attend the public hearing to voice your concerns or support before Council votes.

key_dates.label: Richmond special assessment bylaw public hearing

agenda_briefs.source_body:
P.2
Levy of Special Assessments for Unpaid Administrative Citation and Nuisance Abatement Costs
•  HOLD a public hearing; and ADOPT a resolution authorizing the levy of special assessments against certain properties with unpaid invoices related to administrative citation fees, and nuisance abatement costs pursuant to Richmond Municipal Code Chapters 2.62, 6.38, 9.22, and 11.76 – Community Development Department/Code Enforcement Division (Lina Velasco/ Eva Mann 510-621-1279).

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Richmond zoning and land-use changes to match state housing law
agenda_briefs.summary: Richmond City Council is set to vote on proposed changes to the city's zoning bylaws and General Plan (the city's long-term land-use blueprint). These changes are meant to bring local rules in line with recent updates to California state housing law, as part of the city's 6th Cycle Housing Element. If approved, the amendments would affect several sections of the Richmond Municipal Code (the city's local laws) and the General Plan's land-use and urban design policies. A public hearing will be held before the vote.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Attend the public hearing to voice your support or concerns about the proposed zoning and land-use changes.

key_dates.label: Public hearing on proposed Richmond zoning amendments

agenda_briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Professional Services
agenda_briefs.source_body:
P.1
General Plan and Zoning Text Amendments Related to 6th Cycle Housing Element Program Implementation
•  HOLD a public hearing; INTRODUCE (first reading) an ordinance, as recommended by Richmond’s Planning Commission, amending Articles 15.04.104, 15.04.201, 15.04.202, 15.04.203, 15.04.602, 15.04.607; Sections 15.04.610.180, 15.04.610.360, 15.04.805.040 and 15.04.806.040 of the Richmond Municipal Code (RMC); and ADOPT a resolution, as recommended by Richmond’s Planning Commission, amending the General Plan 2030 Land Use and Urban Design Element, to implement certain programs identified in the City’s adopted 6th Cycle Housing Element to align with recent changes to State Law - Community Development Department (Lina Velasco/Avery Stark 510-620-6714).

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Richmond council to review draft 2026-27 budget and capital plan
agenda_briefs.summary: Richmond City Council is set to consider the draft Fiscal Year 2026-27 Annual Operating Budget and the 2026-31 Five-Year Capital Improvement Plan. As part of this review, council will receive updates from the Community Services, Economic Development, and Fire Departments. This is a proposed budget that has not yet been approved. A final decision will come after all departmental presentations are complete and council discusses the plan.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — this proposed budget could affect city services and fees for your business.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
O.1
Fiscal Year 2026-27 Draft Budget Department Presentations and Budget Updates
•  RECEIVE an update on departmental presentations from Community Services, Economic Development, and Fire Departments as part of the continued review of the draft Fiscal Year (FY) 2026-27 Annual Operating Budget and FY 2026-31 FiveYear Capital Improvement Plan, with discussions upon completion of all departmental presentations; and RECEIVE updated financial and operational considerations – City Manager’s Office/ Finance Department (Shasa Curl/Emily Combs/Mubeen Qader 510-412-2077).

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Richmond council to vote on updated sidewalk vendor rules
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider an ordinance that would update the Sidewalk Vendor Regulations (RMC Chapter 7.42). The proposed changes cover administrative procedures, waiver provisions, and enforcement mechanisms. This item was continued from previous meetings and is now up for first reading — meaning council will introduce the proposed bylaw for discussion and a future vote. If approved, it could affect how and where sidewalk vendors operate in Richmond.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — proposed changes could affect your vendor licence or operations.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
N.13.f
Introduce Ordinance Amending Sidewalk Vendor Regulations (RMC Chapter 7.42)
INTRODUCE an ordinance (first reading) amending Chapter 7.42 of the Richmond Municipal Code (Sidewalk Vendor Ordinance) to update administrative procedures, waiver provisions, and enforcement mechanisms - Public Works Department (Andy Cho 620-6547/Robert Armijo 620-5477). This item was continued from the April 28, 2026, and May 5, 2026, meeting.

agenda_briefs.title: Council to vote on $390,000 electric bikeshare contract amendment
agenda_briefs.summary: Richmond City Council is set to vote on a proposed third contract amendment with Charleston Mobility for the citywide electric bikeshare program. If approved, the amendment would add $390,000 to the existing contract, bringing the total to $3,540,341. This funding would keep the bikeshare program running until the end of the current fiscal year while the city decides the program's long-term future during the Fiscal Year 2026-27 budget process. The money would come from the Environmental and Community Investment Agreement (ECIA) Transportation budget. This item was previously discussed at council meetings on April 28, 2026, and May 5, 2026, and has now been brought back for a decision.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — this could affect transportation options for your customers and employees.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
N.13.e
Contract Amendment No. 3 with Charleston Mobility for Electric Bikeshare Operations
APPROVE a third contract amendment with Charleston Mobility in an amount not to exceed $390,000 for a total not-to-exceed contract amount of $3,540,341, to support the continued operation of the citywide electric bikeshare program till the end of the fiscal year to allow time to determine the future of the program during the Fiscal Year 2026-27 budget process; and APPROPRIATE $390,000 from the Environmental and Community Investment Agreement (ECIA) Transportation budget to cover the cost of the contract amendment – Public Works Department/Community Services Department (Gabino Arredondo 510-620-6606/LaShonda White 510-620-6828).​ This item was continued from the April 28, 2026, and May 5, 2026, meeting.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Richmond Council to vote on $1.28M for Santa Fe Avenue slope fix
agenda_briefs.summary: Richmond City Council will consider a proposal to approve $1,278,126 for the Santa Fe Avenue Slope Stabilization Project. The money would come from three sources: $72,623 transferred from the Santa Rita Road Stabilization Project, $205,502 from a state-funded Quick-Build project, and $1,000,000 from the city's Insurance Reserve Fund. The funds would pay for design work, geotechnical services, and construction. This is a proposed decision — it has not been approved yet and will be voted on at an upcoming council meeting.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
N.13.d
Appropriate Funds for the Santa Fe Avenue Slope Stabilization Project
•  ADOPT a resolution to RATIFY the transfer of $72,623.62 from the Santa Rita Road Stabilization Project; APPROPRIATE $205,502.81 from the Quick-Build project funded through California’s Active Transportation Program for Cutting/Hoffman Project to fund design and geotechnical services; and APPROPRIATE $1,000,000 from the Insurance Reserve Fund to the General Capital Fund in the Fiscal Year 2026–27 budget for construction of the Santa Fe Avenue Slope Stabilization Project - Public Works Department (Hillal Hamdan 510-621-1612/ Daniel Chavarria 510-620-5478).

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Richmond Council to vote on $3.25M road repair project list
agenda_briefs.summary: Richmond Council is set to vote on a proposed resolution that would approve a list of road maintenance and rehabilitation projects for the 2026–27 fiscal year. If approved, the resolution would also authorize spending $3,251,983 from the Senate Bill 1 Road Maintenance and Rehabilitation Account (RMRA) — a special state fund for road repairs. This is a proposed decision and has not yet been approved or enacted.
agenda_briefs.action_required: No action required from business owners at this time. Watch for the council decision — if approved, road work may affect local traffic and access.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
N.13.c
Fiscal Year 2026–27 Senate Bill 1 Road Maintenance and Rehabilitation Fund Project List
•  ADOPT a resolution approving the Fiscal Year 2026–27 Senate Bill (SB) 1 Road Maintenance and Rehabilitation Account (RMRA) project list and APPROPRIATE $3,251,983 in RMRA funds to SB 1 Fund 1019 - Public Works Department (Hillal Hamdan 510-621-1612/Robert Armijo 510-620-5477).

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Richmond Council to vote on Complete Streets funding plan
agenda_briefs.summary: Richmond City Council is set to vote on a proposed funding allocation plan for Complete Streets improvements. This plan would direct money toward pavement management, streetscape upgrades, and other street improvements across the city. The proposal is up for approval at an upcoming council meeting. If approved, the plan would determine how funds are spent on making streets safer and more accessible for everyone, including drivers, pedestrians, and cyclists. The Public Works Department is recommending council approve this funding plan.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
N.13.b
Complete Streets Improvement Elements Project Funding Allocation Plan
•  APPROVE the Complete Streets Improvements (Pavement Management, Streetscape Improvements, etc.) funding allocation plan – Public Works Department (Josef Munoz 510-620-6671/Daniel Chavarria 510-620-5478).

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Council to accept Lincoln Elementary Safe Routes project as complete
agenda_briefs.summary: Richmond City Council is set to vote on a resolution to formally accept the Lincoln Elementary School Safe Routes to School Improvement Project as complete. If approved, this would authorize the city clerk to ratify the previously recorded Notice of Completion (a legal document confirming the project is finished) and authorize the city engineer to release any bonds and retention money held for the project. This is a routine step to close out the project, but it signals that the new pedestrian and cycling safety improvements near the school are ready for use.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
N.13.a
Acceptance of the Lincoln Elementary School Safe Routes to School Improvement Project
•  ADOPT a resolution to ACCEPT the Lincoln Elementary School Safe Routes to School Improvement Project as complete; AUTHORIZE the city clerk to ratify the previously recorded Notice of Completion; and AUTHORIZE the city engineer to execute and release applicable bonds and retention – Public Works Department (Hillal Hamdan 621-1612/Daniel Chavarria 620-5478).

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Richmond council to vote on $7.2M animal control contract
agenda_briefs.summary: Richmond City Council is set to vote on a proposed contract with the Contra Costa County Animal Services Department worth up to $7,200,000. This contract would continue animal control services for the 2026–2027 fiscal year, with an option to extend for three more years (2027–2030). The total contract term would run from July 1, 2022, through June 30, 2030. This item was carried over from the May 5, 2026, council meeting and is now up for approval at an upcoming meeting. If approved, the contract would be managed by the Police Department.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — this contract could affect animal control fees or services for your business.

key_dates.label: Council vote on animal control contract — Richmond

agenda_briefs.source_body:
N.12.c
Agreement with Contra Costa County Animal Services Department for Ongoing Animal Control Services
•  APPROVE and AUTHORIZE a contract with Contra Costa County Animal Services Department in an amount not to exceed $7,200,000 for the continuation of Animal Control Services for Fiscal Year 2026–2027, with an additional three-year term for Fiscal Years 2027–2028, 2028–2029, and 2029–2030, for a total contract term of July 1, 2022, through June 30, 2030 – Police Department (Chief Timothy Simmons 510-621-1802). This item was continued from the May 5, 2026, meeting.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Richmond council to vote on Rod and Gun Club shooting range contract
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed three-year contract with the Richmond Rod and Gun Club for shooting range facility rentals. If approved, the contract would run from July 1, 2026, to June 30, 2029, at a cost not to exceed $25,000 per year (total $75,000). The contract also includes two optional one-year extensions at the same annual rate. This is a sole-source contract, meaning the city would not seek bids from other providers. The decision has not been made yet — council will consider and vote on this proposal at an upcoming meeting.
agenda_briefs.action_required: No action needed from business owners — this is an internal city contract. Watch for the council decision if you use the range.

key_dates.label: Proposed contract start date — Richmond Rod and Gun Club rental
Proposed contract end date — Richmond Rod and Gun Club rental

agenda_briefs.source_body:
N.12.b
Contract with Richmond Rod and Gun Club for Shooting Range Facility Rental
•  APPROVE a three-year sole-source contract with Richmond Rod and Gun Club for shooting range facility rentals in an amount not to exceed $25,000 annually, for a total not to exceed $75,000, for the term July 1, 2026, through June 30, 2029, with two one-year extension options at $25,000 per year – Police Department (Chief Timothy Simmons 510-621-1802).

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Richmond council to vote on $150K GPS contract for police vehicles
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed contract with Verizon Connect to provide GPS tracking and management for police vehicles. The contract would cost up to $150,000 over three years, starting July 1, 2026, with options to extend for two more years at up to $50,000 per year. This is not yet approved — council will decide at an upcoming meeting.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — if approved, this contract will affect police fleet operations starting July 2026.

key_dates.label: Proposed Verizon Connect GPS contract start date — Richmond Police

agenda_briefs.source_body:
N.12.a
Contract with Verizon Connect for Police Vehicle GPS Service
•  APPROVE a contract with Verizon Connect for monthly GPS tracking and management in an amount not to exceed $150,000 over a three-year term from July 1, 2026, to June 30, 2029, plus two one-year extension options not to exceed $50,000 per year – Police Department (Chief Timothy Simmons 510-621-1802).

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Four new stakeholder seats on Macdonald Avenue Task Force
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on adding four new stakeholder positions to the Macdonald Avenue Corridor Task Force. If approved, these seats would be filled through the standard boards and commissions process. The proposal aims to bring more voices to the table for decisions affecting the Macdonald Avenue corridor. The Mayor's Office (Eduardo Martinez) is the contact for questions.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Contact the Mayor's Office (Eduardo Martinez, 510-620-6502) to learn how to apply or nominate someone for a new stakeholder seat.
agenda_briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Retail
agenda_briefs.source_body:
N.11.e
Expansion of the Macdonald Avenue Corridor Task Force Membership
•  APPROVE the addition of four (4) new stakeholder positions to the Macdonald Avenue Corridor Task Force which will be filled through the standard boards and commissions process—Mayor’s Office (Eduardo Martinez (510-620-6502).

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Richmond council committee to rewrite council rules
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider a request to create a special committee (called an 'ad-hoc committee') that would rewrite the City Council Rules of Procedure and Order. The goal is to make council meetings run more smoothly and efficiently. If approved, the mayor would appoint members to this committee, and they would have 60 days to propose changes for the full council to vote on. This is not a done deal yet — it is up for discussion and a vote at an upcoming council meeting.
agenda_briefs.action_required: No action needed from you right now. Watch for the council decision — if the committee is created, proposed rule changes could affect how public input is heard at future meetings.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
N.10.a
Ad-Hoc Committee to Amend City Council Rules of Procedure and Order
•  REQUEST that Mayor Eduardo Martinez appoint a City Council ad hoc committee to work with the city clerk, city attorney, and city manager to develop proposed amendments to the City Council Rules of Procedure and Order and other operational refinements to improve efficiency, for consideration by the full City Council within 60 days of the committee’s appointment - City Clerk’s Office/City Attorney’s Office/City Manager’s Office (Pamela Christian 510-620-6513/Shannon Moore 510-620-6509/Shasa Curl 510-620-6512).

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: 5% salary adjustment for Richmond city clerk in 2026
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider approving a 5% salary increase for the city clerk, effective March 2, 2026. This proposed adjustment is part of the city's executive management agreement with the IFPTE Local 21 union. The decision has not been made yet — it is up for approval at an upcoming council meeting.

key_dates.label: Proposed city clerk salary increase takes effect

agenda_briefs.source_body:
N.9.a
Annual Compensation Adjustment for the City Clerk
•  APPROVE a five percent (5%) salary adjustment for the city clerk effective March 2, 2026, in accordance with the IFPTE Local 21 Executive Management Memorandum of Understanding Section 6.4D – Human Resources Department (Nickie Mastay/Sharrone Taylor 510-620-6602).

agenda_briefs.title: Council to consider wildfire committee recommendations and disband it
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on whether to accept recommendations from the Wildfire Preparedness Ad Hoc Committee to improve the City's wildfire preparedness and mitigation efforts. The committee would then be disbanded. Council will also give direction to staff on which recommendation to fund during the upcoming budget process. This is a proposed decision — nothing has been approved yet.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
N.8.c
Wildfire Preparedness Ad Hoc Committee Recommendations
•  RECEIVE a written presentation from the Wildfire Preparedness Ad Hoc Committee; PROVIDE DIRECTION to staff on the City Council’s preferred recommendation to improve the City’s wildfire preparedness and mitigation efforts during the budget checklist on proposed appropriations; and ACKNOWLEDGE the disbanding of the Wildfire Preparedness Ad Hoc Committee – Wildfire Preparedness Ad Hoc Committee (Chief Aaron Osorio 510-307-8021).

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Richmond council to vote on EMS agreement with Contra Costa County
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed amendment to the interagency agreement with Contra Costa County Health Services for the Emergency Medical Services (EMS) Program. If approved, the amendment would extend the agreement through June 30, 2027, and accept an additional $229,462 in Measure H funding, bringing the total contract payment limit to $458,924. This is a proposed decision that has not yet been enacted.
agenda_briefs.action_required: No action required from business owners at this time — this is a proposed administrative funding decision.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
N.8.b
Interagency Agreement Amendment with Contra Costa County Health Services for Emergency Medical Services Program
•  ADOPT a resolution to: (1) APPROVE and AUTHORIZE the city manager, or designee to execute an amendment to the agreement with Contra Costa County Health Services for Emergency Medical Services Program, extending the term of the current interagency agreement through June 30, 2027; and (2) ACCEPT and APPROPRIATE funds in an amount not to exceed an additional $229,462 in Measure H funding from the Contra Costa County Health Services, bringing the total contract payment limit to $458,924 – Fire Department (Chief Aaron Osorio 510-307-8021/Deputy Chief Rico Rincon 510-307-8041).

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Richmond Council to designate officials for disaster aid applications
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider a resolution to designate specific officials who would be authorized to apply for state and federal disaster financial assistance. This includes applications under the California Disaster Assistance Act and the federal Robert T. Stafford Act. The proposed officials are from the Fire Department. This is a procedural step to ensure the city can quickly access disaster relief funds if needed. The decision has not been made yet.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
N.8.a
Designating Officials Authorized to Execute and Submit Application for Federal and State Financial Disaster Assistance
•  ADOPT a resolution designating officials that are authorized to execute and submit applications to the State of California Governor’s Office of Emergency Services for state financial assistance under the California Disaster Assistance Act and the federal Robert T. Stafford Disaster Relief and Emergency Assistance Act – Fire Department (Shasa Curl 510-620-6512/Emily Combs 510-620-6740/Chief Aaron Osorio 510-307-8021).

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Richmond City Donation Acceptance Policy up for approval
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider a resolution to approve a new Donation Acceptance Policy for the City of Richmond. This policy would set rules for how the city can accept donations from individuals, businesses, or organizations. If approved, it would create a formal process for reviewing and approving donations to make sure they are appropriate and follow city rules. The policy is being brought forward by the Finance Department.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
N.7.c
Donation Acceptance Policy
•  ADOPT a resolution approving the City of Richmond Donation Acceptance Policy – Finance Department (Emily Combs/Jerry Chak 510-620-6569).

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Richmond pension tax override rate set at 0.14% for 2026-27
agenda_briefs.summary: Richmond City Council is set to vote on a proposed ordinance that would set the tax rate for the Pension Tax Override Fund at 0.14% for the 2026-27 fiscal year. This is the first reading of the ordinance, meaning it has been introduced but not yet approved. If passed, this rate would apply to property taxes to fund pension obligations for city employees. The Finance Department is recommending this rate.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — this could affect your property tax rate if you own a business in Richmond.

key_dates.label: Richmond pension tax override ordinance first reading

agenda_briefs.industries: Professional Services, Retail, Landlord & Property Mgmt
agenda_briefs.source_body:
N.7.b
Setting Tax Rate for the Pension Tax Override Fund for Fiscal Year 2026-27
•  INTRODUCE an ordinance (first reading) setting the tax rate for the Tax Override Pension Fund for Fiscal Year 2026-2027 at 0.14 percent – Finance Department (Emily Combs/Mubeen Qader/ Antonio Banuelos 510-620-6741).

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Richmond Council to write off $535,499 in uncollected debts
agenda_briefs.summary: Richmond Council is set to vote on a proposal to write off $535,499.12 in unpaid accounts receivable. 'Write-off' means the city would officially give up trying to collect these debts and remove them from its books. This does not cancel the debt — the city could still try to collect in the future — but it is a signal that the city considers these amounts unlikely to be paid. The proposal covers a mix of delinquent (overdue) and uncollectible accounts from various city services.
agenda_briefs.action_required: No action needed from business owners — this is an internal city accounting decision.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
N.7.a
Approval of Write-Off of Uncollectible Accounts Receivables
•  ADOPT a resolution authorizing the write-off of delinquent and/or uncollectible accounts receivable in the amount of $535,499.12 – Finance Department (Emily Combs/Mubeen Qader/Antonio Banuelos 510-620-6741).

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Richmond council to vote on $60,000 facade grant program
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider approving an extra $60,000 for the Small Business Beautification Facade Improvement Program, run by the Renaissance Entrepreneurship Center. If approved, the total contract would increase to $400,000 and the program would be extended through June 30, 2027. This program helps small businesses pay for building front improvements like new signs, paint, or awnings. The decision has not been made yet — it is up for a vote at an upcoming council meeting.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — if approved, this program could offer grants for your storefront improvements. Contact the Economic Development Department for details.

key_dates.label: Council vote on facade program amendment

agenda_briefs.source_body:
N.6.b
Contract Amendment with Renaissance Entrepreneurship Center for Small Business Beautification Pilot Program
•  APPROVE the first amendment to the contract with Renaissance Entrepreneurship Center to increase the total contract amount by $60,000 for a revised amount not to exceed $400,000, extend the term through June 30, 2027, and support payment administration for eligible businesses participating in the Small Business Beautification Facade Improvement Program - Economic Development Department (Jesson de Leon 510-412-2023/Junne Garcia 510-620-6524/Nannette Beacham 510-620-1306).

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Downtown Richmond property assessment levy for 2026-2027
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a resolution that would allow the City of Richmond to collect a special levy (a mandatory fee) from property owners in the Downtown Richmond Property and Business Improvement District. If approved, this levy would be added to the property tax bills for the 2026-2027 fiscal year. The money would be used to fund improvements and services in the downtown area.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council vote — if approved, this levy will appear on your property tax bill.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
N.6.a
Downtown Richmond Property and Business Improvement District Fiscal Year 2026–2027 Assessment Levy
•  ADOPT a resolution authorizing the City of Richmond to levy assessments on property owners subject to the Downtown Richmond Property and Business Improvement District through the County of Contra Costa property tax rolls for Fiscal Year 2026–2027 – Economic Development Department (Jesson de Leon 510-412-2023/Antonio Banuelos 510-620-6741/Junne Garcia 510-620-6524/Nannette Beacham 510-620-1306).

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Richmond council to accept $35,000 Chevron grant for street outreach
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider a resolution to accept and appropriate a $35,000 grant from the Chevron Community Engagement Foundation. The money would go to the Office of Neighborhood Safety (ONS) to enhance street outreach and mobile crisis response. This is a proposed decision — it has not yet been approved. If passed, the funds would support community safety programs in Richmond.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council vote — if approved, this grant could fund safety outreach that may affect local businesses and neighbourhoods.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
N.5.a
Grant Award from Chevron Community Engagement Foundation
•  ADOPT a resolution to ACCEPT and APPROPRIATE a $35,000 grant award from the Chevron Community Engagement Foundation for the Office of Neighborhood Safety (ONS) to Enhance Street Outreach/Mobile Crises Response – Community Services Department (LaShonda White/Sam Vaughn 510-620-5404).

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Richmond council to vote on $50,000 housing contract with Makanai
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider approving a three-year, on-call contract with Makanai, LLC for up to $50,000. The company would provide budget and reporting support for past housing programs. This is a proposed decision — it has not been approved yet and will be voted on at an upcoming council meeting.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
N.4.b
Contract with Makanai, LLC for Budget and Reporting Support for Housing Programs
•  APPROVE an on-call contract with Makanai, LLC to provide budget and reporting support related to past housing programs in an amount not to exceed $50,000, for a three-year term with a term expiration of June 30, 2029, with one one-year extension option – Community Development Department (Lina Velasco/Jesus Morales 510-620-6706).

agenda_briefs.title: Council to vote on on-call hearing officer contracts up to $300,000 per firm
agenda_briefs.summary: Richmond Council is set to vote on a proposal to award on-call contracts to two law firms — Nastich Law and Robert M. Snider, Attorney at Law — to provide Administrative Hearing Officer services. These are independent legal experts who hear and decide disputes about local bylaws, such as property or licensing issues. If approved, each firm could be paid up to $300,000 over a three-year term. The decision is up for approval at an upcoming council meeting.
agenda_briefs.action_required: No action needed from business owners at this time — watch for the council decision, which may affect how bylaw disputes are handled.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
N.4.a
On-Call Contracts with Nastich Law and Robert M. Snider, Attorney at Law for Administrative Hearing Officer Services
•  ADOPT a resolution authorizing on-call contracts with Nastich Law and Robert M. Snider, Attorney at Law, to provide Administrative Hearing Officer services, for an amount not to exceed $300,000 per firm, for a three-year term - Community Development Department (Lina Velasco/ Eva Mann 510-621-1283).

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Richmond Council to vote on fee waiver for WCCUSD Latino/x graduation
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider a proposal to waive all fees and staffing costs for the West Contra Costa Unified School District's Latino/x Graduation Ceremony at the Richmond Memorial Auditorium in 2026. If approved, the city manager would be authorized to spend city resources — including overtime for police, fire, public works, and other departments — to support the event. This is a proposed decision that has not yet been voted on.
agenda_briefs.action_required: No action required from business owners at this time. Watch for the council vote to see if the waiver is approved.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
N.3.a
Waiver of Auditorium Fees, Staffing Costs, and Other Associated Costs to Support the West Contra Costa Unified School District Latino/x Graduation at the Richmond Memorial Auditorium and Convention Center
•  REQUEST the city manager issue fee waivers to West Contra Costa Unified School District for the use of the Richmond Memorial Auditorium in 2026 for the Latino/x Graduation Ceremony or Ceremonies, and AUTHORIZE the city manager or their designee to expend City resources to support the graduation, which may include overtime from Police, Fire, Public Works, Community Services, and other departments as required  - Councilmember Claudia Jimenez (510-620-6565).

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Richmond council to vote on $110,000 legal research contract
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed contract with Thomson Reuters for legal research subscription services. If approved, the contract would provide the City Attorney's Office with access to Westlaw and CoCounsel Legal for a three-year term starting July 1, 2026, at a cost not to exceed $110,000. This is a proposed decision that has not yet been approved.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
N.1.c
Contract with Thomson Reuters Enterprise for Legal Research Subscription Services
•  APPROVE a contract with Thomson Reuters for legal research subscription services for Westlaw and CoCounsel Legal in an amount not to exceed $110,000 for the term of July 1, 2026, through June 30, 2029, subject to non-substantive revisions approved by the City Attorney’s Office; and AUTHORIZE the city manager or their designee to execute the contract – City Attorney’s Office (Christopher Dykzeul 510-620-6507/Shannon Moore 510-620-6509).

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Richmond council to vote on $150K investigation contract
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider approving a three-year legal services agreement with Ellis Investigations to conduct confidential personnel investigations for the City of Richmond. The proposed contract would run from July 1, 2026, through June 30, 2029, and would not exceed $150,000. This is a proposed decision — it has not been approved yet and is up for a vote at an upcoming council meeting.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
N.1.b
Legal Services Agreement with Ellis Investigations for Confidential Personnel Investigations
•  APPROVE a legal services agreement with Ellis Investigations to conduct confidential personnel investigations for a term of July 1, 2026, through June 30, 2029, for a total amount not to exceed $150,000 – City Attorney’s Office/Human Resources Department (Shannon Moore/Nickie Mastay/Sharrone Taylor 510-620-6509).

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Richmond council to vote on $75,000 increase for Winehaven legal fees
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider approving a fourth amendment to the legal services agreement with the law firm Nevers, Palazzo, Packard, Wildermuth & Wynner, PC. This would increase the total contract amount by $75,000 — from $375,000 to $450,000 — for legal work related to the Winehaven Legacy litigation and related matters. The proposed amendment would also extend the contract term through December 31, 2027. If approved, the $75,000 would come from the city's General Fund (the main operating budget). This is a proposed decision and has not yet been voted on or enacted.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council vote — if approved, this will increase city legal spending from the General Fund.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
N.1.a
Legal Services Agreement No. 4 with Nevers, Palazzo, Packard, Wildermuth & Wynner, PC
•  APPROVE a fourth amendment to the legal services agreement with Nevers, Palazzo, Packard, Wildermuth & Wynner, PC, for legal representation related to the Winehaven Legacy litigation and related matters, to increase compensation by $75,000 for a total contract amount not to exceed $450,000, extend the term through December 31, 2027, and APPROPRIATE $75,000 from the General Fund to cover associated legal fees. – City Attorney’s Office (Kimberly Chin 510-620-6709/Shannon Moore 510-620-6505).
